title: Why You Can't Tell Where You End and Your Phone Begins
hook: Your mind is a crystal. Whatever you put in front of it, you become. Look at your screen time.

script.visual: Clear quartz crystal held up. A red flower behind it — the crystal looks red. A blue cloth — the crystal looks blue. Close-up on the crystal.
script.voiceover: Your mind is a crystal. It takes on the color of whatever you put behind it.

script.visual: Person holding their phone. TikTok opens. Quick cuts of content reflecting onto their face in color. Their expression morphs with each video.
script.voiceover: Sutra 1.41. Patanjali's image — not mine. He says when the mind gets quiet enough — kshina vritti, the noise dies down — it becomes samapatti. Absorbed. It takes on the exact shape of whatever it is looking at. And here is the part that should scare you: this is already happening. All day. You are just not choosing the object.
script.text_overlay: SAMAPATTI = the mind becomes what it beholds

script.visual: Person scrolling at 11:47 PM. Blue light on their face. Quick montage: outrage clip, thirst trap, conspiracy edit, doom headline.
script.voiceover: You open the app at 11:47 PM. Ninety minutes later you close it. You are angry. You are horny. You are anxious. You are convinced the world is ending. You did not choose any of those states. The feed chose them. And your crystal mind obediently took on every single color.
script.text_overlay: The feed picked your state. Not you.

script.visual: Person closes the phone, sits up, looks at one object — a candle, a breath, a single word. Lighting stabilizes.
script.voiceover: Patanjali's move is not renunciation. He is not telling you to throw the phone away. He is asking: what are you putting behind the crystal? Because you are going to become it either way. Pick the object. A breath. A sentence you love. A person you want to be. The crystal does not care. It will take on any color you hand it.
script.text_overlay: You become what you behold. Pick carefully.

script.visual: Wide shot. Person sitting quietly. The crystal on a table catches warm evening light.
script.voiceover: Samapatti is not a mystical state. It is a physics of attention. What you look at, you become.
script.text_overlay: What you look at, you become.

script.voiceover: What color did your crystal take on in the last hour? Be honest.
script.text_overlay: Last hour: what color was your crystal?

cta: What was the last thing you put in front of your crystal mind — and what color did it turn? Comment below.
caption:
Your mind is a crystal. It takes on the color of whatever is behind it.

Sutra 1.41: क्षीणवृत्तेरभिजातस्येव मणेर्ग्रहीतृग्रहणग्राह्येषु तत्स्थतदञ्जनता समापत्तिः
kshina-vritter abhijatasyeva maner grahitr-grahana-grahyeshu tat-stha-tad-anjanata samapattih

"When the mental noise quiets, the mind — like a transparent crystal — takes the color of the knower, the knowing, and the known. This is samapatti."

You think you are choosing what to think about. You are not. You opened TikTok at 11:47 PM and by 1:15 AM you were angry, anxious, and convinced everyone else has their life figured out.

The feed picked your color. Your crystal obeyed.

Patanjali is not telling you to renounce the phone. He is asking one surgical question: what are you putting behind the crystal? Because you are going to become it either way.

Pick the object carefully. You are what you behold.

#yogasutras #patanjali #samapatti #attentioneconomy #doomscrolling #digitalminimalism #mindfulness #consciousness #yogaphilosophy #ancientwisdom #yogadarshan #phoneaddiction #mentalhealth #focus #crystalmind
music_suggestion: Start minimal — single glass-like chime. At the TikTok montage, layer anxious electronic texture that shifts color with each cut. Dissolve into a sustained crystalline drone for the closing.
